zh.lifeSummary: Agnes Neves是William Neves的女兒，於1562年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可拉文哈姆。她於1584年12月28日與William Paine在英國英格蘭素福爾可拉文哈姆結婚。她去世於1645年10月8日在她的家鄉享年83歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可拉文哈姆聖 彼得 安德 聖 包利斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Neves
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Luis, Fernando, Gilberto, Mario, Ricardo, Augusto, Horacio, Joaquin.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Agostinho, Catarina, Manoel, Paulo.
Portugese: from a short form of the Marian title and name Maria das Neves ‘Mary of the Snows’ (see Nieves ).
Galician and Asturian-Leonese: habitational name from any of the places called Neves in the provinces of Pontevedra, Lugo, and A Coruña (all three in Galicia), and Asturias.

variants.name: Nieves
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Miguel, Carlos, Juan, Angel, Rafael, Francisco, Jesus, Luz, Manuel, Ramon.

Spanish: from a short form of the Marian title and name María de las Nieves ‘Mary of the Snows’, given particularly to children born on 5 August, on which date the Virgin allegedly once caused it to snow in Rome. The surname may also be a reference to a local church dedicated to the Virgin Mary bearing this title.
Spanish: possibly also a nickname from the plural of nieve ‘snow’, for someone with snow-white hair.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Agnes Neves was born in 1562, in Lavenham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of William Neves. She married William Paine on 28 December 1584, in Lavenham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. She died on 8 October 1645, in her hometown, at the age of 83, and was buried in St Peter And St Paul's Church, Lavenham, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Agnes Neves est née en 1562, à Lavenham, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fille de William Neves. Elle a épousé William Paine le 28 décembre 1584, à Lavenham, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle est décédée le 8 octobre 1645, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 83 ans, et a été enterrée à St Peter And St Paul's Church, Lavenham, Suffolk, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Nevesは1562年にイギリスイギリススフォルクラベンハムでWilliam Nevesの娘として生まれた。1584年12月28日にイギリスイギリススフォルクラベンハムでWilliam Paineと結婚した。彼女は1645年10月8日に故郷で83歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクラベンハムスト ペター アンド スト プアウル'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.St Peter And St Paul's Church, Lavenham,: 영국 수폴크 레이븐함 스트 피터 앤드 스트 폴'스 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Neves(은)는 1562년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 레이븐함에서 William Neves의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1584년 12월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 레이븐함에서 William Paine(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1645년 10월 8일 그녀의 고향에서 83세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 레이븐함 스트 피터 앤드 스트 폴'스 처르치에 묻혔다.

ru.St Peter And St Paul's Church, Lavenham,: ст питир анд ст поул'с чурч, лавенам, суффолк, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Agnes Neves родилась в 1562 г. в местности лавенам, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. Её отца звали William Neves. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Paine 28 декабря 1584 г. в местности лавенам, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. Она умерла 8 октября 1645 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 83 лет и была похоронена в местности ст питир анд ст поул'с чурч, лавенам, суффолк, Англия.
